volumeTitle: Persians. Seven against Thebes. Suppliants. Prometheus Bound
label: Seven against Thebes (Section 3.625 - Section 795)

sections.label: Section 3.625
sections.original: θεοῦ δὲ δῶρόν ἐστιν εὐτυχεῖν βροτούς .
sections.translation: Still it is God ’ s gift when mortals succeed . Exit Lasthenes .

sections.label: Section 3.626
sections.translation: Gods , hear our just prayers and fulfil them , that the city may have good fortune ! Turn aside the evils suffered in war onto those who invade our land ! May Zeus strike them

sections.label: Section 3.630
sections.translation: with his thunderbolt outside the walls and slay them !

sections.label: Section 631
sections.original: τὸν ἕβδομον δὴ τόν τ [*] ἐφ ἑβδόμαις πύλαις
sections.translation: Last I will tell of the seventh champion , him at the seventh gate , [*] your own brother , and of what fate he prays for and calls down on the city . His prayer is that after he mounts the battlements and is proclaimed king in the land ,

sections.label: Section 635
sections.translation: and shouts the paian in triumph over its capture , he may then meet you in combat , and once he kills you , that he may perish at your side , or , if you survive , make you pay with banishment in the same way as you dishonored him with exile . Mighty Polynices shouts such threats and

sections.label: Section 640
sections.translation: invokes his native gods , the gods of his fatherland , to watch over his prayers in every way . He holds a shield , a perfect circle , newly-made , with a double symbol cleverly fastened on it :

sections.label: Section 645
sections.translation:
a woman modestly walking in the fore leads a man in arms made , it appears , of hammered gold . She claims to be Justice , as the lettering indicates , I will bring this man back and he will have his city and move freely in his father ’ s halls .
Such are the inventions fixed to their shields .

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: σὺ δ αὐτὸς ἤδη γνῶθι τίνα πέμπειν δοκεῖ :
sections.translation: Quickly determine yourself whom you think it best to send . Know that you will find no fault with me in the substance of my report , but you yourself determine on what course to pilot the city . Exit .

sections.label: Section 653
sections.original: ὦ θεομανές τε καὶ θεῶν μέγα στύγος ,
sections.translation: O my family sired by Oedipus , steeped in tears ,

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: ὤμοι , πατρὸς δὴ νῦν ἀραὶ τελεσφόροι .
sections.translation: driven to madness by the gods and by the gods detested ! Ah , now indeed our father ’ s curses are brought to fulfillment . But neither weeping nor wailing is proper for me now , lest a grief even harder to bear is brought to life . As for him whose name is so very fitting , Polynices , we shall know soon enough what the symbol on his shield will accomplish ,

sections.label: Section 660
sections.translation: whether the babbling letters shaped in gold on his shield , together with his mind ’ s wanderings , will bring him back . If Justice , Zeus ’ s maiden daughter , were attending his actions and his thoughts , this might be so . But as it is , neither when he escaped the darkness of his mother ’ s womb ,

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: οὔτ ἐν τροφαῖσιν , οὔτ ἐφηβήσαντά πω ,
sections.translation: nor in childhood , nor at any point in his early manhood , nor when the beard first thickened on his cheek , did Justice acknowledge him and consider him worthy . And even now I do not think that she is standing by his side to aid the destruction of his fatherland .

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: ἢ δῆτ ἂν εἴη πανδίκως ψευδώνυμος
sections.translation: Indeed , Justice would truly be false to her name , if she should ally herself with a man so utterly audacious in his plans . Trusting in this fact I will go and stand against him — I myself in person . Who else has a more just claim ? Commander against commander , brother against brother ,

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: ἐχθρὸς σὺν ἐχθρῷ στήσομαι . φέρ ὡς τάχος
sections.translation: enemy against enemy , I will take my stand . Quick , bring my greaves to protect against spears and stones !

sections.label: Section 677
sections.original: μή , φίλτατ ἀνδρῶν , Οἰδίπου τέκος , γένῃ
sections.translation: No , son of Oedipus , most dear of our men , do not be like in temperament to him who is called by such an evil name . It is enough that Cadmeans

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: ἐς χεῖρας [*] ἐλθεῖν : αἷμα γὰρ καθάρσιον .
sections.translation: are advancing to close combat with Argives . That bloodshed can be expiated . But when men of the same blood kill each other as you desire , the pollution from this act never grows old .

sections.label: Section 683
sections.original: εἴπερ κακὸν φέροι τις , αἰσχύνης ἄτερ
sections.translation: If indeed a man should suffer evil , let it be without dishonor , since that is the only benefit for the dead .

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: κακῶν δὲ κᾀσχρῶν οὔτιν εὐκλείαν ἐρεῖς .
sections.translation: But you cannot speak of any glory for happenings that are at once evil and held in dishonor .

sections.label: Section 1.686
sections.original: τί μέμονας , [*] τέκνον ; μή τί [*] σε θυμοπλη-
sections.translation: For what are you so eager , child ? Do not let mad lust for battle fill your soul and carry you away . Reject this evil passion while it is still young .

sections.label: Section 1.689
sections.original: ἐπεὶ τὸ πρᾶγμα κάρτ ἐπισπέρχει θεός ,
sections.translation: Since God hastens the deed so urgently ,

sections.label: Section 1.690
sections.original: ἴτω κατ οὖρον κῦμα Κωκυτοῦ λαχὸν
sections.translation: let the whole race of Laius , hated by Phoebus , be swept on the wind to Cocytus ’ destined flood !

sections.label: Section 1.692
sections.translation: A savage desire eats away at you , drives you to murder , blood-sacrifice proscribed by divine law , whose only fruit is bitterness .

sections.label: Section 1.695
sections.original: φίλου γὰρ ἐχθρά [*] μοι πατρὸς μέλαιν [*] ἀρὰ
sections.translation: True , my own beloved father ’ s hateful , ruinous curse hovers before my dry , unweeping eyes , and informs me of benefit preceding subsequent death . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.698
sections.original: ἀλλὰ σὺ μὴ ποτρύνου : κακὸς οὐ κεκλή-
sections.translation: No , do not let yourself be driven to it . You will not be called a coward if you retain life nobly . Will not the avenging Erinys in her dark aegis

sections.label: Section 2.700
sections.original: εἶσι δόμων Ἐρινύς , ὅταν ἐκ χερῶν
sections.translation: leave your house , when the gods receive sacrifice from your hands ?

sections.label: Section 2.702
sections.translation: The gods , it seems , have already banished us from their care , yet they admire the grace we offer them when we perish . So then , why should we cringe and shy away from deadly fate ?

sections.label: Section 2.705
sections.original: νῦν ὅτε σοι παρέστακεν : ἐπεὶ δαίμων
sections.translation: It is only at this moment that death stands close by you , for the divine spirit may change its purpose even after a long time and come on a gentler wind . But now it still seethes .

sections.label: Section 2.709
sections.translation: Yes , the curses of Oedipus have made it seethe in fury .

sections.label: Section 2.710
sections.translation: Too true were the phantoms in my sleeping visions , predicting the division of our father ’ s wealth !

sections.label: Section 712
sections.original: πιθοῦ [*] γυναιξί , καίπερ οὐ στέργων ὅμως .
sections.translation: Obey us women , although you do not like to .

sections.label: Section 713
sections.original: λέγοιτ ἂν ὧν ἄνη τις : [*] οὐδὲ χρὴ μακράν .
sections.translation: Recommend something that can be accomplished ; your request need not be lengthy .

sections.label: Section 714
sections.original: μὴ ʼλθῃς ὁδοὺς σὺ τάσδ ἐφ ἑβδόμαις πύλαις .
sections.translation: Do not yourself take the road to the seventh gate !

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: τεθηγμένον τοί μ οὐκ ἀπαμβλυνεῖς λόγῳ .
sections.translation: Let me assure you , you will not blunt my sharpened purpose with words .

sections.label: Section 716
sections.original: νίκην γε μέντοι καὶ κακὴν τιμᾷ θεός .
sections.translation: And yet any victory , even a cowardly one , is nonetheless held in honor by God .

sections.label: Section 717
sections.original: οὐκ ἄνδρ ὁπλίτην τοῦτο χρὴ στέργειν ἔπος .
sections.translation: A soldier must not embrace that maxim .

sections.label: Section 718
sections.translation: But are you willing to harvest the blood of your own brother ?

sections.label: Section 719
sections.original: θεῶν διδόντων οὐκ ἂν ἐκφύγοις [*] κακά .
sections.translation: When it is the gods who give you evils , you cannot flee them . Exit .

sections.label: Section 1.720
sections.translation: I shudder in terror at the goddess who lays ruin to homes , a goddess unlike other divinities , who is an unerring omen of evil to come . I shudder that the Erinys invoked by the father ’ s prayer will fulfil the over-wrathful

sections.label: Section 1.725
sections.translation: curses that Oedipus spoke in madness . This strife that will destroy his sons drives the Erinys to fulfillment .

sections.label: Section 1.727
sections.translation: A stranger distributes their inheritance , a Chalybian immigrant from Scythia , a bitter divider of wealth ,

sections.label: Section 1.730
sections.translation: savage-hearted iron that apportions land for them to dwell in , as much as they can occupy in death when they have lost their share in these wide plains .

sections.label: Section 2.734
sections.translation: But when both have died , each killing

sections.label: Section 2.735
sections.translation: the other in mutual slaughter , and the earth ’ s dust has swallowed the black streams of their blood , who could offer sacrifice that might make purification ? Who could cleanse them of their pollution ?

sections.label: Section 2.740
sections.translation: O , the new troubles of this house mixed with its evils of before !

sections.label: Section 2.742
sections.translation: Indeed I speak of the ancient transgression , now swift in its retribution . It remains even into the third generation ,

sections.label: Section 2.745
sections.translation: ever since Laius — in defiance of Apollo who , at his Pythian oracle at the earth ’ s center , said three times that the king would save his city if he died without offspring —

sections.label: Section 3.750
sections.translation: Ever since he , overcome by the thoughtlessness of his longing , fathered his own death , the parricide Oedipus , who sowed his mother ’ s sacred field , where he was nurtured ,

sections.label: Section 3.755
sections.translation: and endured a bloody crop . Madness united the frenzied bridal pair .

sections.label: Section 3.758
sections.original: κακῶν δ ὥσπερ θάλασσα κῦμ ἄγει :
sections.translation: Now it is as if a sea of evils pushes its swell onward . As one wave sinks , the sea raises up another ,

sections.label: Section 3.760
sections.translation: triple-crested , which crashes around the city ’ s stern . In between a narrow defense stretches — no wider than a wall . I fear that the city will be overthrown along with its kings .

sections.label: Section 4.766
sections.translation: For the compensation is heavy when curses uttered long ago are fulfilled , and once the deadly curse has come into existence , it does not pass away . When the fortune of seafaring merchants has grown too great ,

sections.label: Section 4.770
sections.translation: it must be thrown overboard .

sections.label: Section 4.772
sections.translation: For whom have the gods and divinities that share their altar and the thronging assembly of men ever admired

sections.label: Section 4.775
sections.translation: so much as they honored Oedipus then , when he removed that deadly , man-seizing plague from our land ?

sections.label: Section 5.778
sections.translation: But when , his sanity regained , he grew miserable in his wretched

sections.label: Section 5.780
sections.translation: marriage , then carried away by his grief and with maddened heart he accomplished a double evil . With the hand that killed his father he struck out his eyes , which were dearer to him than his children .

sections.label: Section 5.785
sections.translation: Next he launched brutal , wrathful words against the sons he had bred — ah ! curses from a bitter tongue — that wielding iron in their hands they would one day divide his property .

sections.label: Section 5.790
sections.translation: So now I tremble in fear that the swift-running Erinys will bring this to fulfillment .

sections.label: Section 792
sections.translation: Take heart , you daughters who were nurtured by your mother . Our city has escaped the yoke of slavery ; the boasts of the powerful men have fallen to the ground .

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: πόλις δ ἐν εὐδίᾳ τε καὶ κλυδωνίου
sections.translation: The city enjoys fair weather and has taken on no water even though it has been buffeted by many waves . The walls hold , and we have fortified the gates with champions fully capable in single-handed combat . For the most part all is well , at six of the gates .
